title: Frontend and Backend Contract Alignment
purpose: Lock the implemented browser-facing parent-backend contracts to the stable frontend account surfaces by adding a durable contract inventory, focused regression coverage, and only the smallest safe DTO or routing corrections required to make those contracts honest. This spec intentionally does not build new public marketing/pricing APIs, admin dashboards, new persistence models, or any new Pay commercial logic.

context.summary: The roadmap and implementation plan point to frontend/backend contract alignment as the next workstream after the verified background-jobs/security foundation. Repo reality shows the authenticated application surface is already broad enough to justify a contract-honesty slice: versioned `/api/v1` routers exist across auth, settings, dashboard, launcher, billing, support, announcements, service status, rewards, and Discord integrations; schema-level contract tests already exist for several domains; but there is no single durable frontend-contract inventory for the implemented account surfaces, contract descriptors exist only for select settings routes, and public pricing/product-page contracts from the architecture docs are still unimplemented and should be documented as future rather than invented.
repo_reality_summary: Repo searches confirmed the canonical authenticated surface is already mounted under `/api/v1` via `app/api/routers/v1/__init__.py` and `app/main.py`, with concrete routers in `app/api/routers/v1/auth.py`, `profiles.py`, `addresses.py`, `communication_preferences.py`, `dashboard.py`, `launcher.py`, `billing.py`, `support.py`, `announcements.py`, `service_status.py`, and `integrations.py`, plus the nested rewards router in `app/api/routers/v1/rewards.py`.

The existing route-registration regression in `tests/unit/test_router_registration.py` already proves most browser-facing paths exist, including `/api/v1/dashboard/summary`, `/api/v1/launcher/products`, `/api/v1/billing/*`, `/api/v1/support/tickets`, `/api/v1/integrations/discord*`, and `/api/v1/rewards/me/*`, so the gap is less about route creation and more about durable contract inventory and response-shape regression depth.

Current contract-descriptor endpoints are uneven: hidden `/_contract` routes exist for profiles, addresses, and communication preferences in `app/api/routers/v1/profiles.py`, `addresses.py`, and `communication_preferences.py`, but there is no comparable durable contract artifact for auth, dashboard, launcher, billing, support, announcements, service status, integrations, or rewards.

Schema-focused regression coverage already exists for the application foundation and several frontend page surfaces in `tests/unit/test_application_layer_foundation.py`, `tests/unit/test_dashboard_launcher_billing_schemas.py`, `tests/unit/test_support_announcements_status_schemas.py`, and the domain API tests such as `test_dashboard_api.py`, `test_billing_api.py`, `test_support_api.py`, `test_discord_integration_api.py`, `test_rewards_summary_api.py`, `test_reward_objectives_api.py`, `test_reward_notifications_api.py`, and `test_reward_badges_api.py`.

The only existing durable contract inventory doc found in the repo is rewards-specific: `docs/architecture/Rewards_API_Application_Inventory.md`, enforced by `tests/unit/test_rewards_api_inventory.py`. There is no equivalent cross-domain frontend contract inventory covering the implemented authenticated account surfaces.

Pydantic DTOs under `app/schemas/` already encode several contract conventions the frontend depends on: shared success and error envelopes in `app/schemas/common.py`, nested ownership-oriented billing and launcher payloads in `app/schemas/billing.py` and `app/schemas/launcher.py`, and cursor pagination reuse in support, announcements, and billing schemas. Those conventions should be locked by broader contract regression tests rather than rewritten wholesale.

Repo searches also confirmed an important scope boundary: the frontend contract map mentions public product/pricing/about surfaces, but there are no public pricing/catalog/testimonial routers or schema modules in the current repo. This spec should document those as future or not implemented, not invent runtime APIs for them.

global_guardrails: Do not expand scope beyond this spec.
Do not add dependencies unless John explicitly approves them.
Do not duplicate Pay commercial business rules in parent.
Do not store sensitive payment details in parent.
Do not build admin dashboards unless explicitly scoped.
Do not redesign stable frontend pages.
Do not make Discord linkage affect rewards or product access unless the spec explicitly says so.
Do not allow frontend APIs to directly award points/rewards/badges.
Do not return raw ORM objects from routers.
Do not mark incomplete items complete.
Append progress entries to the absolute end of `progress/progress.txt`.
Run `python -m compileall app tests alembic` before marking implementation items complete.
Run `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from test` before marking implementation items complete.
Keep this workstream contract-focused: prefer inventory docs, DTO-safe response adjustments, hidden contract helpers, and regression tests over broad service or repository rewrites.
Do not implement new public pricing, product-page, testimonial, or marketing-content APIs in this spec; document them as future or not implemented if they appear in the frontend contract map.
Do not add new database models, Alembic migrations, queue infrastructure, or external integrations for this workstream.
If a contract gap can be resolved by clarifying documentation or tests instead of changing runtime behavior, prefer the documentation/test fix.

items.title: Author a Durable Frontend Contract Inventory for Implemented Account Surfaces
items.description: Create a cross-domain architecture inventory that maps the already implemented frontend-facing authenticated/account surfaces to concrete backend endpoints, request/response DTOs, ownership labels, fallback behavior, auth requirements, email-verification requirements, and Pay dependency notes. The inventory should cover auth, settings/profile, addresses, communication preferences, dashboard, launcher, billing, support, announcements, service status, rewards, and Discord integrations, while explicitly marking public pricing/product/about contracts as future or not implemented.
acceptance_criteria: A durable contract inventory document exists under `docs/architecture/` and references the real router, schema, and test files currently in the repo instead of inventing future runtime paths.

For each implemented page or modal surface covered by this spec, the inventory records endpoint, method, request shape, response shape, ownership source, fallback behavior, auth requirement, email-verification requirement, and Pay dependency status in the format required by the contract-map docs.
The inventory explicitly distinguishes already implemented authenticated surfaces from public or future surfaces that are still unimplemented, including public pricing/product/about-page APIs.
The inventory does not redefine Pay commercial truth and does not introduce new runtime behavior by itself.

items.title: Add Cross-Domain Contract Regression Coverage for Stable Frontend Payloads
items.description: Add or extend focused unit tests that lock the stable browser-facing contract surfaces to the DTO and routing conventions the frontend depends on. The target is contract honesty: route registration, response envelope shape, cursor pagination reuse, mutation-success conventions, ownership-oriented payload nesting, and safe-field constraints across the implemented account surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests assert the canonical `/api/v1` route surface for the implemented frontend account pages and modals without reopening unrelated runtime domains.

Regression coverage exists for representative payloads across dashboard, launcher, billing, support, announcements, service status, rewards, Discord integration, auth/session, and settings surfaces, using the current DTO modules under `app/schemas/`.

Tests explicitly lock shared contract conventions such as standard error envelopes, mutation-success responses, cursor pagination shapes, and safe DTO behavior so future edits cannot silently leak sensitive fields or flatten ownership boundaries.
The new tests can fail on real contract drift even if route handlers still import successfully; they are not limited to smoke assertions.

items.title: Close Minimal Contract Gaps Revealed by the Inventory and Regression Harness
items.description: Make only the smallest safe runtime or schema adjustments needed to bring implemented frontend-facing surfaces into alignment with the documented contracts and new regression tests. Favor DTO-safe fixes, hidden contract helpers, and router/schema clarifications over broad behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria: Any runtime changes remain limited to already implemented frontend-facing routers and schemas under `app/api/routers/` and `app/schemas/`, with no repository, worker, migration, or new integration work.

If a surface lacks a durable contract expression needed by the tests or inventory, the implementation adds the lightest-weight solution that fits existing patterns, such as a hidden contract helper route, a response DTO cleanup, or explicit ownership/fallback metadata.
Billing and launcher contract fixes preserve the Pay boundary by documenting or exposing safe fallback states without adding local commercial truth.
No new public pricing/product/testimonial APIs are implemented, and Discord linkage remains unrelated to rewards or product access.

items.title: Verify Frontend and Backend Contract Alignment
items.description: Run the required verification suite after the contract inventory, regression coverage, and any minimal contract-gap fixes are in place. Only mark this spec complete when the new contract slice is fully documented, covered, and verified.
acceptance_criteria: The final state includes both the durable contract inventory and cross-domain regression coverage for the implemented frontend account surfaces.
Only fully completed items are marked complete, and `progress/progress.txt` is appended at EOF.

completion_definition: A durable frontend/backend contract inventory exists for the implemented account surfaces.
Relevant tests are added or updated to lock route registration, DTO shape, pagination, mutation-success, and safe-field behavior.
No unrelated dirty files are introduced.
No incomplete item is marked complete.
